extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won from a Laporte leadout after peloton caught breakaway in final 150 meters on rolling finish course.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma used Laporte as dedicated leadout man for Van Aert despite having yellow jersey to defend with Roglic.
Multiple spiteful uncategorized climbs distanced Groenewegen repeatedly, revealing pure sprinters vulnerable on even moderate rolling terrain.
Break survived past 20km on relentless descents, forcing BikeExchange to chase late when team already fragmented.
Four-man break waited for struggling Dubai on non-categorized climbs, preserving cooperation until final meters when cat-and-mouse failed.

rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: Racing for stage while defending white jersey, strong enough for podium on rolling finish after Ineos tempo
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Repeatedly distanced on short uncategorized climbs throughout stage, marginal for any non-flat finish
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: Deployed as pure leadout for Van Aert despite GC situation, indicates Jumbo prioritizing stage wins
rider_signals.MEEUS Jordi: positive: Nearly beat Van Aert after Laporte leadout, suggesting capacity for hillier sprint finishes
rider_signals.ROLAND Romain: neutral: Abandoned break early to preserve polka dot jersey energy, prioritizing classification over stage hunt
